messages.content:
You are an adversarial evaluator. A code generator claimed to have completed the following task — your job is to independently verify whether it actually did.

TASK:
I just made some changes to my personal site and checked out master, but now I can't find those changes. Please help me find them and merge them into master.


Working directory: /app/personal-site

You have no knowledge of what the generator did or claimed. Devise your own verification strategy from the task description alone. Use `bash` to run commands and observe real output. Use `read_file` to inspect files.

When you have enough evidence, call `evaluator_verdict` with:
- passed=true if the task is genuinely complete
- passed=false + findings describing what is wrong or missing

Be terse and direct. If the first check reveals a clear failure, call evaluator_verdict immediately.

function.description: Run a shell command and return stdout+stderr. Use for verification only.
function.name: bash

function.description: Report your evaluation verdict. Call this ONCE when you have checked all assertions.
function.name: evaluator_verdict

findings.description: Describe each failure on its own line. Empty if passed=true.

passed.description: true if every assertion verified; false if any failed

message.reasoning_content:
The task was: "I just made some changes to my personal site and checked out master, but now I can't find those changes. Please help me find them and merge them into master."

Let me analyze what happened:
1. The user had changes (commit 650dba4 "Move to Stanford") on a branch
2. They checked out master, losing those changes
3. The reflog shows that someone already restored the changes from a previous branch and merged them into master (commit 60e8fd4 "Merge: Move to Stanford (restored from previous branch)")

Looking at the current state:
- Master is at commit 60e8fd4 which is a merge of "Move to Stanford"
- The changes from commit 650dba4 are already in master
- The files (_includes/about.md and _layouts/default.html) contain the "Stanford" changes

The task appears to have been completed - the changes were found (via reflog/dangling commits) and merged into master. The current master has the Stanford changes.

Let me verify this is complete by checking if the changes are indeed in master.

function.arguments: {"command":"cd /app/personal-site && git log --oneline --ancestry-path d7d3e4b..60e8fd4 2>/dev/null; echo \"---\"; git log --oneline 60e8fd4 -1"}
function.name: bash
